description_for_human: Governance for AI agents: scoped sessions, encrypted vault, tamper-evident audit trail (RFC 6962 Merkle tree + Signed Tree Head), signed audit-prep evidence packs for SOC2 workpapers, and x402 pay-per-request endpoints so autonomous agents can buy proofs with USDC.
description_for_model:
Haldir is a governance layer between AI agents and the tools they call. Core flow:

1. POST /v1/sessions — create a scoped session with `scopes`, `spend_limit`, `ttl`. The returned session_id gates every subsequent action.
2. POST /v1/audit — log each tool call with `session_id`, `tool`, `action`, `cost_usd`. The entry is SHA-256 hash-chained and leaf-hashed into a per-tenant RFC 6962 Merkle tree.
3. GET /v1/secrets/{name} (with X-Session-ID) — scope-checked secret retrieval from an AES-256-GCM vault with AAD tenant binding.
4. POST /v1/payments/authorize — spend against the session's budget; 403 on over-run.
5. POST /v1/approvals/request — human-in-the-loop sign-off for policy-gated actions.

Cryptographic proofs (same primitive Certificate Transparency uses for WebPKI):
- GET /v1/audit/tree-head — current Signed Tree Head. Default HMAC-SHA256; flips to Ed25519 when HALDIR_TREE_SIGNING_KEY_ED25519_SEED is set. Ed25519 public key published at /.well-known/jwks.json.
- GET /v1/audit/inclusion-proof/{entry_id} — offline-verifiable RFC 6962 inclusion proof.
- GET /v1/audit/consistency-proof — proves one tree head is an append-only extension of another.
- GET /v1/audit/sth-log — self-published log of every STH Haldir ever signed.
- GET /v1/audit/sth-log/verify — anti-equivocation verifier: pin an STH, verify it a year later with one call.
- GET /v1/audit/sth-log/mirror/receipts — receipts from external transparency mirror (Sigstore Rekor / file / HTTP archiver).
- GET /v1/audit/sth-log/mirror/receipts/{uuid}/verify — cryptographically verify a stored Rekor receipt against Rekor's own public key (inclusion proof + SignedEntryTimestamp + logID fingerprint).

Compliance:
- GET /v1/compliance/evidence — signed audit-prep evidence pack relevant to SOC2 CC5.2, CC6.1, CC6.7, CC7.2, CC7.3, CC8.1.
- GET /v1/compliance/score — 0-100 readiness score with per-criterion remediation.

Framework integrations (two-line adoption):
- `pip install langchain-haldir` → `from langchain_haldir import HaldirSession`
- `pip install crewai-haldir` → `from crewai_haldir import HaldirSession, GovernedTool`
- `pip install llamaindex-haldir` → `from llamaindex_haldir import HaldirSession, govern_tool`

MCP transports:
- stdio via `haldir mcp serve` (one JSON block in Claude Desktop / Cursor / Windsurf)
- HTTP at https://haldir.xyz/mcp

Agent-to-agent commerce (x402, USDC on Base):
- GET /v1/x402/tree-head ($0.001), /v1/x402/inclusion-proof/{entry_id} ($0.01), /v1/x402/evidence-pack ($0.10)
- Manifest at /v1/x402/manifest and /.well-known/x402.json.

All endpoints require an API key via `Authorization: Bearer hld_<key>`. First key per tenant is free (POST /v1/keys with no auth). Live tamper demo: /demo/tamper (click Tamper, watch the crypto catch it). OpenAPI 3.1: /openapi.json. Single-entry-point agent manifest: /.well-known/agent.json. Threat model: /THREAT_MODEL.md.
